Conner Major Shawn Holloway M1300 SW 14th Street Bentonville, Arkansas 72712 Phone: 479-271-1011 (Detention) 479-271-1008 (Admin) May 26, 2015 RE: Mine Resistant Ambush Protected vehicle acquired through 1033 Program To Whom It May Concern: On April 29, 2014, the Benton County Sheriffs Office obtained a Mine Resistant Ambush Protected vehicle (MRAP) through Law Enforcement Support Office 1033 Program. As of that date, our office has implemented a policy for the operation and training of the MRAP vehicle. The purpose of obtaining this vehicle is for it to be utilized by the Benton County Sheriff's Office Special Weapons and Tactics Team for the protection of officers and citizens during high risk operations for the safety of persons from threats such as, but not limited to potential gunfire. The policy has guidelines for the operation and maintenance of the vehicle. A deputy that has NOT received the appropriate training will not be allowed to operate the MRAP vehicle. Furthermore, drivers will have (at a minimum) an Arkansas Class B Commercial driver s license. The training will consist of the following: Y Overview of the vehicle to include its capabilities and limitations v Characteristics of the vehicle so the operators will have knowledge of its size and weight Basic maintenance Safe operating speeds Movement and maneuver Ground Guiding techniques for safe operation in small areas due to limited visibility Operation on slopes Restricted terrain Rough terrain driving Vehicle tip and rollover Steering and proper maneuvering Mount and Dismount Falls, smashed, and pinched extremities Use of seatbelts Risk of contact with low hanging objects such as power lines SS & SSS SEAR Detention Investigations Patrol Special Operations Captain Jeremy Guyll Captain Kenneth Paul Captain Lynn Hahn Captain Andy Lee Jr.
Key_Detail: As of that date, our office has implemented a policy for the operation and training of the MRAP vehicle

Department: Benton County Sheriff s Office POLICIES AND PROCEDURES SUBJECT MRAP Operations EFFECTIVE NUMBER 411.3 6/1/15 6/1/16 ISSUE DATE | 6/1/15 REVISION The Benton County Sheriff's Office obtained a Mine Resistant Ambush Protected (MRAP) Vehicle through the LESO 1033 Program. The purpose of this vehicle is that it will be utilized by the Benton County Sheriff's Office for the protection of law enforcement officers and citizens during high risk operations for the safety of persons from threats such as but not limited to potential gunfire. Additional uses of this vehicle will be to assist citizens during natural disaster events to navigate through extreme terrain or to drive through damaged areas to assist citizens affected by such disasters. Scheduled Review Date APPROVED BY PURPOSE: 1. Mine Resistant Ambush Protected Vehicle (MRAP) i. Vehicle designed to transport personnel and/or equipment at the scene of a tactical operation while providing ballistic protection for the occupants. ii. Assigned operators will keep an inventory of all equipment stored in the MRAP and will notify the Team or Assistant Team Leader to replace items as needed due to use. iii. _ Only approved team member(s) will be responsible for driving the MRAP to call- outs and ensuring that it is maintained, well kept, and organized. 2. MRAP Guidelines i. Training requirements: 8-hour basic operator course and 4 hours a year for primary and alternate operators. Operators will also possess a valid Arkansas Class B Commercial Driver s License. ii. Ground guide not required during tactical deployment; recommended under all other conditions. iii. Pre-trip inspection prior to normal operation; may be disregarded under tactical deployment, if monthly check has been performed. Po Page Toh? Effective Date: 8/1/14 Subject; MRAP Number: 411.3 Operations Benton County Sheriff's Office

Department: vi. Monthly inspection, to include running the motor while parked for one hour - System check to be performed during weekly inspection to include security. Code III response to any deployment site is not authorize without TTC approval. Parking or storage: when not deployed or at maintenance facility, the MRAP must be parked in an area designated by the commander at the Benton County Sheriff s Office. MAINTENANCE AND STORAGE: Li: The vehicle shall be secured from public access during storage. This can be accomplished minimally by locking doors, turning off all power systems, and/or by storage in a secure building or structure from public. The vehicle will be kept maintained in a safe and sustainable operating condition and will be in a response capable condition at all times. Any conditions identified during inspection and maintenance that may render the vehicle unsafe for use shall require that the vehicle be taken out of service, tagged, and notification made to the SWAT Commander and/or Operations Commander immediately. The vehicle shall not be used until such time that it has been repaired and/or rendered safe to operate. TRAINING AND OPERATION: I Zi, No personnel will be allowed to operate the vehicle without training. The training may be completed by in-service staff instructors, who have completed a train-the-trainer course, or by a contracted company or trained military member who has extensive experience or qualifications with this type of vehicle. Training shall include classroom instruction and a hands on / drivers training components. The training shall at a minimum consist of the following: Overview of the vehicle to include its capabilities and limitations. Characteristics of the vehicle so the operators will have knowledge of its size and weight. Basic Preventative Maintenance Checks & Services (PMCS). Safe operating speeds. Steering, movement and maneuvering. Visibility concerns. Operation of controls and indicators. Ground guide techniques for safe operation. Operation on slopes. Restricted terrain. Rollover hazards and egress procedures. Mount and dismount. Falls, smashed, and pinched extremities. Use of seatbelts. Risks of contact with low hanging objects such as power lines. Page 2 of 2 Subject: MRAP Operations Effective Date: 8/1/14 Number: 411.3 Benton County Sheriff's Office

Department: Benton Police Department Policy: Effective: 4/24/2015 Date of Origin: 4/24/2015 Armored Rescue Vehicle (ARV) Operations I. Purpose A. This policy provides clear guidelines for the safe and effective deployment of the Benton Police Department s Armored Rescue Vehicle (ARV). Il. Policy A. It shall be the policy of the Benton Police Department to develop and implement a policy that provides guidelines on the manner in which the ARV is deployed and maintained. B. The policy will also contain guidelines that establish a mandated training program for all personnel responsible for the ARV s operational deployment. All department personnel shall recognize and adhere to the guidelines set forth within this policy. Il. Procedures A. ARV Deployment 1. The Benton Police Department s ARV is assigned as a tactical unit to the SWAT team to be utilized in the mitigation of tactical situations requiring its use. More specifically, the ARV is intended to be deployed during tactical situations that require additional protective measures for police officers, who have encountered or might encounter gunfire or other hazardous situations during the performance of their duties. . The Benton Police Department may also deploy the ARV to assist the citizens of our jurisdiction during natural disaster events that require the delivery of police services or rescue measures, to include extreme terrain or high water events. . In the event the ARV is deployed under the tenets of mutual aid, the Benton Police Department will always provide personnel trained to operate the ARV. BNPD personnel shall have the final decision regarding the safe and appropriate use of the ARV. All requests for deployment of the ARV must be approved by the Chief of Police or his designee. B. ARV Operation Ly The Benton Police Department s ARV may only be operated by personnel within the agency having been specifically trained to operate and maneuver the ARV ina safe and effective manner.

Department: CABOT POLICE DEPARTMENT CABOT, ARKANSAS 72023 Mine Resistant Ambush Protected Vehicle (MRAP) This policy is for internal use only and does not enlarge an employee s civil liability in any way. The policy should not be construed as creating a higher duty of care, in an evidentiary sense, with respect to third party civil claims against employees. A violation of this policy, if proven, can only form the basis of a complaint by this department for non-judicial administrative action in accordance with the laws governing employee discipline. Applicable Arkansas Statutes: Date Implemented: I. Purpose: Mine Resistant Ambush Protected Vehicle (MRAP) A vehicle designed to transport personnel and/or equipment to the scene of a tactical operation while providing ballistic protection for the occupants. Additional uses of this vehicle will be to assist the citizens of Cabot during natural disaster events to navigate through extreme terrain or drive over damaged areas to assist citizens affected by such disasters. I. Policy: . An inspection, to include running the motor while parked for one hour, will be quarterly. . A system check and security check will be performed quarterly. . A pre-trip inspection prior to normal operation is required. This may be disregarded under a tactical deployment. . A ground guide (Back-up spotter) is not required during tactical deployment but recommended under all other conditions, including training. . Code Three response to any deployment site is not authorized without the approval of the Chief of Police or his designee. An escort Police Unit will be utilized anytime the MRAP is in use while driving any public roadway. . When not deployed or at a maintenance facility, the MRAP must be parked in an area approved by the Chief of Police or his designee. H. An eight (8) hour basic operator course will be required to operate the MRAP. After the initial operator course is completed, all SORT Members/Operators will be required an additional four (4) hours of annual training. a oOo AW > Q

The purpose of this vehicle is to be utilized by the Craighead County Special Weapons and Tactics team for the protection of officers during high risk operations for the safety of such officers from potential gunfire while in the performance of their duties. Logistical Sustainment Group. All training curriculum used in training is derived from the Center for Army Lessons Learned published by Maneuver Center of Excellence at Fort Benning, titled MRAP Observations, Insight, and Lessons and BAE Systems MRAP Instructor Guide. Department: Hot Springs Police Department S.W.A.T. Training Course Summary Sheet Course Title: MRAP Operations Purpose of the Course: To familiarize Hot Springs Police Department SWAT Operators with basic MRAP functions, preventative maintenance, and driving operations of the MRAP. Objectives: At the end of the block of instruction the students will be able to successfully: 1. Identify the Power Plant and transmission, as presented by the Instructor. 2. Identify the control panel and identify all gauges and lights, as well as, switches, as presented by the Instructor. 3. Identify the routine maintenance required, as presented by the Instructor. 4. Demonstrate familiarity with the passenger capacity, as well as load weights, as presented by the Instructor. 5. Demonstrate familiarity with the Vehicle Operations, as presented by the Instructor. 6. Demonstrate familiarity with vehicle recovery operations, as presented by the Instructor. Certification: Eric Wacaster is a police officer and SWAT Team member with the Hot Springs Police Department. He is a certified police and firearms instructor through the Commission on Law Enforcement Standards. He is also a certified Force on Force instructor through General Dynamics. Ofc. Zac Rostan will assist with instruction. Ofc. Rostan is a police officer and SWAT team member with Hot Springs Police Department as well as a staff sergeant in the United States Army Reserve. Ofc. Rostan is a veteran of multiple overseas deployments where he has operated MRAPs in theater. He has also trained multiple other soldiers in the use and operation of MRAPs. 2

Department: LITTLE ROCK POLICE DEPARTMENT DIVISIONAL OPERATING PROCEDURE AVIATION UNIT DISTRIBUTION: REPLACES: NUMBER: DIVISIONAL NEW 5600-12 DATE: 04/26/2012 I. Purpose A. B. Cc. Dz. The magnitude of demands for police service increases as today s society becomes more mobile and affluent. It becomes more evident that rapid mobility, providing swiftness of response, in all areas of police service is essential. The growing complexity of police work has resulted in an increase in specialization and some modification in the previously accepted concept of patrol duties. The successful implementation of the preventative patrol concept requires adaptation of new techniques to our patrol efforts. The objectives of the program are directly related to the insertion of helicopters into the patrol function. The following examples serve to emphasize the desired objective: 1. Supporting and coordinating patrols and the protection of life and property; 2. Traffic control and surveillance; 3. Crime prevention; 4. Criminal surveillance; 5. Search and rescue; 6. Special surveillance details; 7. Apprehension of offenders; and, 8. Cover for ground elements. The above examples of objectives to be accomplished have one factor in common. Cooperation between ground units and aircraft is essential. The capabilities of the patrol car and aircraft can do much to achieve these goals only when they are used in a coordinated effort to support each other. It is evident, therefore, that the success of this program requires the active support of every member of our Department. Accountability - The Aviation Unit is supervised by the Aviation Unit Sergeant, The Aviation Unit Sergeant is also the Special Events Coordinator who is directly responsible to the Aviation Unit Lieutenant, who is the Special Units Commander. The Aviation Unit Lieutenant is directly responsible to the Headquarters Division Commander. Headquarters Division 5600-12-1 Divisional Operating Procedure

Department: Il. Responsibility A. 10. ll. 12. Headquarters Division The Aviation Unit observers are supervised by the Aviation Unit Sergeant. The Aviation Unit Sergeant will be available as needed on a part-time basis unless special mission assignment details his/her additional presence. The Aviation Unit s hours of assignment will be varied according to the needs of the Department. In the event that an observer or alternate is not available for duty for any reason, the Aviation Unit Sergeant will amend his/her hours to man the shift. Aviation Unit Sergeant Responsibilities Be familiar with and obey all General Orders, Directives, and Rules and Regulations of the Little Rock Police Department, Coordinate and formulate plans for the operation of the Unit s aircraft and related activity; Keep the Aviation Unit Lieutenant informed of all operational phases of the Aviation Unit; Acquaint all personnel under his supervision with such orders, directives, and policies requiring compliance; Be aware of the training needs of the members of the Unit and ensure that the necessary training is provided; Closely evaluate personnel assigned to the Unit during their training, in accordance with existing procedures; Assume the responsibility for the supervision of all assigned duties to ensure the efficient and proper performance of such duties; Develop and implement a public relations program that will tend to achieve public acceptance and approval of the Departmental use of the aircraft; Ensure that required reports are properly submitted by himself and members of the Aviation Unit; Establish and maintain an effective relationship with the FAA; Assist in the formulation of plans for the operation of the Aviation Unit; Maintain schedules and supervise activity of the part-time observers, pilots and mechanic assigned to the Unit. 5600-12-2 Divisional Operating Procedure

B. Observer Responsibilities 1, General a) The part-time observers are assigned to the Headquarters Division for operational purposes and report to the Aviation Unit Sergeant. b) Prepare and submit written reports, as required, by proper authority. 2. Function a) Ascertain operational assignments and coordinate _ this information with the pilot. b) Become knowledgeable with available information concerning any assigned flight. c) Assure that all necessary auxiliary equipment is operational and properly stowed on board the aircraft. d) Be alert for any condition that could affect the safe operation of the aircraft. e) Ensure that all police communications equipment is operational. 3. Observer In-Flight Responsibilities Headquarters Division a) Be alert for aircraft, obstructions, or unsafe conditions, which may affect the safe operation of the aircraft. b) Comply with the directions from the pilot on any matter pertaining to the safe operation of the aircraft. c) Operate auxiliary equipment as required. (1) Required equipment: An operable fire extinguisher, first aid kit and emergency locator transmitter (ELT) shall be kept in each aircraft at all times while the aircraft is being used. 5600-12-3 Divisional Operating Procedure

Department: (1) The Headquarters Division Commander or in his absence, the Section Commander, may approve requests for aircraft service in cities contiguous to Little Rock. (2) Requests should be limited to police emergencies and extended car chases. A police emergency is defined as an on-going, life threatening situation, where the aircraft can be of definite, positive assistance. b) Non-Contiguous Cities - Requests for aircraft service in non- contiguous cities must be approved by the Division Commander or Bureau Commander unless an emergency situation exists. c) The Aviation Unit Lieutenant and the Aviation Unit Sergeant will be notified as soon as possible of any newsworthy or sensational incidents involving the aircraft s use outside the City Limits of Little Rock. d) At no time will a request for aircraft service outside the City of Little Rock be approved if the aircraft is needed within the City. IV. Local Flying Regulations A. General 1, Purpose - The purpose of this section is to provide information and guidance, prescribe general policies to all Police Department personnel utilizing the facilities at Airports, and further establish local flying regulations for all flights by Police Department personnel within the City of Little Rock. 2. Scope a) Policies and procedures contained in this section are directive in nature and supplement existing directives of the Department and Federal Air Regulations. b) Additions, deletions, and amendments will be published as required. Recommendations for revisions should be submitted to the Headquarters Division Commander. c) Violations or deviations from these regulations, including forced landings, must be reported to the Headquarters Division Commander. B. Flight Regulations 1. Aircraft Clearances - Flights conducted under a local flight clearance are subject to the following restrictions: a) Must adhere to Visual Flight Rules (VFR) at all times; Headquarters Division 5600-12-11 Divisional Operating Procedure

Department: K. Miscellaneous 1. No officer will fire any weapon from the aircraft. 2. Doors may be removed from the aircraft only with an Aviation Unit s supervisor s approval. If removal for an emergency reason should become necessary, the Quick Release Pin should remain with the door. Proper care should be taken in removing and placing the doors in a secured area to prevent any damage. Appropriate airspeed limitations will be complied with. Vv. Procedures for Radio Service Calls A. Aviation Unit personnel shall respond to all requests for service made by members of the Police Department. B. When on regular patrol, Aviation Unit personnel will respond to any call where the aircraft can be of use to the officers on the ground or assist in the arrest of a suspect. Cc. Effective communications is essential with the officers on the ground. VI. Reports A. Shift Summary Reports are due daily as needed upon mission activity or an emergency call-out basis detailing the flight(s), specific activity conducted, etc... Vil. Annual Review A. The Sergeant of the Aviation Unit will do an annual review of the Unit. B. The Annual Report is due in December with the other end of the year reports for the Headquarters Division. c. An assessment will be made to determine the effectiveness of the program and the need for additional Aviation Unit personnel. Headquarters Division 5600-12-17 Divisional Operating Procedure

Department: County of Mississippi State of Arkansas SHERIFF S DEPARTMENT 685 North CR599 Luxora, Arkansas 72358 870-762-2243 FAX 870-658-2510 Mission #1 is to Protect and Serve AUBRY D. COOK SHERIFF December 17, 2014 The Mississippi County Sheriff's Department has received a Mine Resistant Ambush Protected Vehicle (MRAP) via the LESO 1033 program. The MRAP will be utilized by the Special Response team of the Mississippi County Sheriff's Department for the protection of its officers during high risk operations where such officers may be subjected to gunfire while in the performance of their duties. The vehicle will also be utilized for other purposes such as significant weather events and natural disasters where the off road capabilities of the vehicle can be utilized to assist the citizens of Mississippi County during such disasters. The vehicle may also be used in public relations events to allow the public to observe the capabilities of the vehicle. The Mississippi County Sheriff's Department has also implemented guidelines and training for the operation and maintenance of the vehicle. No officer will be allowed be allowed to operate the vehicle until he or she has completed a training course derived from military technical manuals and training materials and manufacturers manuals and training materials on the operation and maintenance of the vehicle. Training topics are included but not limited to the following: e An overview of the vehicle s capabilities and limitations. e Characteristics of the vehicle so that operators have knowledge of its size and weight. e Basic Maintenance Safe operation speeds and steering maneuvers e Use of Ground guides due to limited visibility e Operation on slopes e Rough terrain driving e Vehicle tip and rollover hazards e Falls, smashed and pinched extremities. e Rollover and Egress procedures. e Use of Seatbelts * Overhead obstacles such as risks of contact with overhead power lines.

Department: Pulaski County Office of the Sheriff Doc Holladay, Sheriff 2900 S. Woodrow St., Little Rock, AR 72204 Tel: (501) 340-6600 Fax: (501) 340-7080 June 8, 2015 Mr. James Ray, Law Enforcement Support Office RE: MRAP James: As of 6/1/15, the Pulaski County Sheriff's Office (PCSO) has implemented a policy for the operation and training of the Mine-Resistant Ambush Protected (MRAP) vehicle obtained from the Law Enforcement Support Office (LESO). The purpose of the vehicle is for use by the PCSO Special Weapons and Tactics (SWAT) Team in the protection of personnel during high risk operations by providing safe cover from potential gunfire while in the performance of their duties. Additional uses of this vehicle are to assist the citizens of Pulaski County during natural disaster events to navigate through extreme terrain or traverse over damaged areas when aiding citizens affected by such disasters. The policy contains guidelines for the operation and maintenance of the vehicle, based upon curriculum used in training established by the Center for Army Lessons Learned and published by the Combined Arms Center at Fort Leavenworth, Kansas, titled MRAP Vehicle Tactics, Techniques ai and Procedures. The training will consist of the following: 1. Overview of the vehicle to include capabilities and limitations. 2. Vehicle characteristics. 3. Maintenance. 4. Safe operating speeds. 5. Vehicle steering, movement and proper maneuvering. 6. Ground guiding techniques due to limited visibility. 7. Operations of slopes. 8. Restricted terrain. 9. Rough terrain driving. 10. Vehicle tip over and roll-over, to include egress procedures. This training will include both classroom and practical hands-on exercises. Sincerely, Doc Holladay > Pulaski County Sheriff DH:so ce: File

Department: MIKE MCGOUGH \ 250 AMERICAN ROAD SHERIFF EL DORADO, ARKANSAS 71730 BILL HICKMAN PHONE 870-864-1970 CHIEF DEPUTY Fax 870-864-1992 June 1, 2015 Re: Mine Resistant Ambush Protected (MRAP) Vehicle To Whom It May Concern: The Union County Sheriff's Office has implemented a policy for the operation and training of the MRAP vehicle obtained through the LESO/1033 program. The purpose of this vehicle is to be used by the Union County Sheriff's Office Special Response Team for the protection of officers during high risk operations for the safety of such officers from potential gunfire while in the performance of their duties. Additional use of the MRAP will be to assist the citizens of Union County during natural disaster events to navigate through extreme terrain or to drive over damaged areas to assist the citizens who are affected from the disasters. The policy has guidelines for the operation and maintenance of the vehicle. It states no officer will be allowed to operate the MRAP without any training. The training will be conducted by a trained military member who has extensive training in use and operation by the United States Army and also has used the MRAP during deployments. All training curriculum used in training is derived from the Center for Army Lessons Learned published by the Combined Arms Center. The training will consist of the following: -Overview of the vehicle to include its capabilities and limitations -Characteristics of the vehicle so the operator will know the size and weight -Basic maintenance -Safe operating speeds -Movement and maneuver -Ground guiding techniques for safe operation in small areas due to limited visibility -Operation on slopes -Restricted terrain -Rough terrain driving
